summary: Soundboard capture of a stellar Feb‑73 show with an epic Dark Star/Eyes/Half‑Step and sizzling Big Railroad Blues, though the mix has Billy’s Ride overly forward.
review: The performance is widely praised – Jerry is described as “extremely inspired,” the Dark Star > Eyes of the World > Half Step stretch is called “great,” and highlights like China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, Playing in the Band and Big Railroad Blues are repeatedly lauded.  The SBD source is generally clear but several listeners note that Billy Kreutzmann’s ride cymbal sits too far forward in the right channel and that a few tracks are missing from the archive copy.  Overall the show ranks among the best of Winter ’73 despite those technical quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong, consistent praise for musical performance (70% weight) and a decent soundboard source with only mix‑level issues (30% weight) yields an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear SBD capture, but Billy's ride cymbal is mixed too loud in the right channel and some songs are missing from the archive version, causing occasional listening fatigue.

standout_songs: Dark Star > Eyes of the World > Half Step
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
Going Down The Road Feeling Bad
poor_songs: Not Fade Away (mix issues, unlistenable ending)
show_quality.setlist_flow: A long, varied first set followed by a high‑energy second set; the flow is praised for its momentum despite a few missing tracks in the archived version.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as extremely inspired; solos on Greatest Story and Big Railroad Blues are highlighted as beauties.
band_member_comments.Phil: Generally solid bass work but missed two cues during Tennessee Jed, causing a brief stumble.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for sharp playing and vocal contributions throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy Kreutzmann’s ride cymbal is mixed too loud in the right channel, making it tedious at times.
